pi_items.source: setup_task.sh (where it creates the repo and uploads the artifacts) and export_result.sh (where it checks if it's deleted)
task.json description and export_result.sh (which evaluates task success by checking this exact repo name)
task.json description and export_result.sh (which checks if the created repo is of type 'maven')
setup_task.sh (constructs upload path using TARGET_PATH and JAR_FILE) and export_result.sh (checks this exact path in the target repo)
setup_artifactory.sh (ADMIN_USER variable) and curl commands in setup_task.sh
setup_artifactory.sh (ADMIN_PASS variable) and curl commands in setup_task.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real Apache Commons Lang jar file downloaded from Maven Central. The initial state is set up with an Artifactory user 'admin' and password 'password'. A temporary generic repository named 'temp-uploads' is populated with the artifact at the path 'org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'. The agent is expected to create a new Maven repository named 'libs-commons-local', move the artifact into it, and delete the original 'temp-uploads' repository. All parameters (user, repos, types, and paths) are confirmed by the environment setup and verification scripts.
